sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) folly (from the root אָוַל). Very frequently in the Proverbs, as, 5:23 12:23 13:16 14:17 , 18 Pro 14:18, 29 Pro 14:29 15:2 , 14 Pro 15:14, 21 Pro 15:21.

(2) impiety, Psa 38:6 comp. נְבָלָה, 69:6 .

(3) perhaps power, pre-eminence; as from the root אוּל No. 2, 3, Pro 14:24, אִוֶּלֶת בְּסִילִים אִוֶּלֶת “the pre-eminence (or great honours) of fools are folly,” i.e. a fountain of foolish actions. The writer appears to have played on the double signification of the word אִוֶּלֶת.
